case_title: O.K. INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. PLANNING DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN et al
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: PROOF OF SERVICE OF MOTION DEMURRER FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS PLANNING DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS PLANNING DEPARTMENT OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
OPPOSITION TO DEFTS DEMURRER TO THE VERIFIED FIRST AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF ADMINSTRATIVE MANDATE FILED BY PETITIONER O.K. INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING INSPECTION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 2006 NOV, PETITIONER FAILED TO EXHAUST ITS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES SO THE COURT LACKS JURISDICTION TO GRANT THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE PETITION. AS TO THE 2007 PERMIT APPLICATION, THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE PETITION IS MOOTED BY RESPONDENT'S REACTIVATION OF THAT PERMIT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MADISON S. SPACH JR. OF SPACH, CAPALDI & WAGGAMAN, LLP, TEL. NO. (949)852-0710 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. CELIA W. LEE, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3858 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING INSPECTION'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is sustained without leave to amend. As to the 2006 NOV, Petitioner failed to exhaust its administrative remedies so the Court lacks jurisdiction to grant the relief sought in the Petition. As to the 2007 Permit Application, the relief sought in the Petition is mooted by Respondent's reactivation of that Permit. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK
